bullets: Orbs (Defect): each orb has a Passive that repeats and an Evoke when the orb is removed.
Channel places an orb in the right-most empty slot; if all slots are full, the right-most orb is Evoked first, then the new orb is placed.
Ingress order: array index 0 is the right-most slot (first for channel/evoke resolution in this ordering); higher indices are to the left.
Lightning, Frost, Dark passives trigger at end of turn; Plasma passive triggers at start of turn.
Focus improves Lightning, Frost, and Dark orb numbers shown below; Plasma is unaffected by Focus.
Dark: passive_amount/evoke_amount from the mod reflect scaled damage; Dark grows each end of turn and Evoke hits the lowest-current-HP enemy (leftmost on ties).
Lock-On (e.g. Bullseye): affected enemy takes 50% more damage from Lightning and Dark orbs.

Lightning.passive_detail: End of turn: deal passive_amount damage to a random enemy (includes Focus).
Lightning.evoke_detail: Evoke: deal evoke_amount damage to a random enemy (includes Focus).

Frost.passive_detail: End of turn: gain passive_amount Block (includes Focus).

Dark.short: Scaling damage orb — lowest HP target on evoke.
Dark.passive_detail: End of turn: increases stored damage (mod reports in passive_amount / related fields; includes scaling).
Dark.evoke_detail: Evoke: deal current stored damage to enemy with lowest HP (leftmost if tied).

Plasma.short: Energy orb — not scaled by Focus.
Plasma.passive_detail: Start of turn: gain Energy from passive (mod value; typically 1).
Plasma.evoke_detail: Evoke: gain Energy (mod value; typically 2). Plasma ignores Focus.
